channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vernon Kay and Tess Daly announce amicable separation after 25 years of marriage
item.description: Television presenters Vernon Kay and Tess Daly have announced their decision to separate after 25 years of marriage. The couple, who share daughters Phoebe, 21, and Amber, 16, released a joint stateme...

item.title: Australia's social classes and the myth of egalitarianism
item.description: Despite the national myth of egalitarianism, class divisions in Australia are alive and well, according to ANU research conducted a decade ago. The study identifies six social classes in contemporary ...

item.title: Government allows private equity and pension funds to bid for highway PPP projects
item.description: In a significant policy shift, the Indian government has now allowed venture capital, private equity, infrastructure, and pension funds to participate in bidding for highway projects under the Public-...

item.title: Kouga Dam Overflow Forces Evacuations in Gamtoos River Valley Amid Heavy Rains
item.description: Heavy rainfall in the Eastern Cape and parts of the Southern Cape has caused the Kouga Dam to overflow, prompting urgent evacuations along the Gamtoos River valley. Over a 48-hour period, certain area...

item.title: Sarah Sherman’s dating life and relationship with Dan Sloan explained
item.description: Sarah Nicole Sherman, better known as Sarah Squirm, is a talented American comedian, actress, and screenwriter, currently part of the Saturday Night Live (SNL) cast. At 33 years old, Sarah tends to ke...

item.title: Princess Margriet of the Netherlands Attends Ottawa Tulip Festival Opening
item.description: Princess Margriet of the Netherlands made a ceremonial visit to Ottawa to mark the start of the annual Canadian Tulip Festival, an event celebrated for its colourful displays and cultural significance...

item.title: Quezon City launches upgraded skate park and public tennis courts at Quezon Memorial Circle
item.description: Quezon City has officially opened its new competition-standard skate park and public tennis courts located at the Quezon Memorial Circle. According to Mayor Joy Belmonte, these facilities are part of ...

item.title: ASUS ROG Announces Strix OLED XG34WCDMS Ultrawide Gaming Monitor and XG129C Touchscreen Secondary Display
item.description: ASUS Republic of Gamers (ROG) has introduced two new additions to its gaming monitor lineup: the ROG Strix OLED XG34WCDMS and the ROG Strix XG129C. The flagship XG34WCDMS is a premium 34-inch ultrawid...

item.title: Macron Says France Rejected Naval Operation to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said France did not seriously consider a naval deployment to force the Strait of Hormuz back open. He described the idea of a military operation to reopen the strategi...

item.title: Government Measures to Control Inflation Including Work-from-Home and Restrictions on Foreign Weddings
item.description: The news article discusses the measures announced by Prime Minister Narendra Modi to curb the rising prices in India. The government has recommended work-from-home policies wherever feasible to reduce...

item.title: Supreme Court ruling to increase electricity expenses for Indian Railways
item.description: The Indian Railways is likely to face increased electricity expenses following a Supreme Court ruling which affirmed that it qualifies as a 'consumer' under the Electricity Act, 2003. This means the n...

item.title: How Kenyan Celebrities Honoured Mothers on Mother’s Day
item.description: On Sunday, May 10, 2026, Mother’s Day was celebrated across Kenya with celebrities and politicians expressing their appreciation for mothers, wives, and mother figures on social media. Kennedy Rapudo ...

item.title: Four Canadians Returning from Cruise with Hantavirus to Self-Isolate in B.C.
item.description: Four Canadian passengers from a cruise ship experiencing a hantavirus outbreak are returning to Canada on a chartered flight. They are expected to land in Bagotville, Quebec, before being transferred ...

item.title: Rave Wins 125,000 Pesos on Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal
item.description: On the popular game show Kapamilya Deal Or No Deal, contestant Rave successfully challenged the odds and won 125,000 pesos. In an intense round, Rave strategically played his briefcase, showing both c...

item.title: Disturbing Online Trend in Brazil Encourages Violence Against Women Who Reject Advances
item.description: A disturbing trend has emerged on Brazilian TikTok, in which young men record videos simulating violent attacks against women who reject their romantic advances. These videos, which often begin as see...

item.title: Safa open to Hugo Broos continuing as Bafana coach if he delays retirement
item.description: Safa’s Technical Committee Chair, Jack Maluleke, confirmed that Hugo Broos will still be considered for the Bafana Bafana coaching role should he decide not to retire after the 2026 Fifa World Cup in ...

item.title: Highest paid NHL players for the 2026/27 season and their salaries
item.description: This article highlights the top 10 highest-paid players in the National Hockey League for the 2026/27 season. Kirill Kaprizov of the Minnesota Wild tops the list with a cap hit of $17 million, making ...

item.title: Iran Responds to US Ceasefire Proposal Amid Ongoing Conflict in Lebanon and Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Iran has formally responded to the latest US ceasefire proposal via Pakistani mediators, emphasising that negotiations should prioritise a permanent end to the ongoing conflict. The Iranian government...

item.title: Camarines Sur lawmaker, DPWH officials, and contractors face charges over failed flood control structures
item.description: A lawmaker from Camarines Sur, Rep. Arnulf Bryan Fuentebella, together with officials from the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) and private contractors, is now facing legal complaints fo...

item.title: Modi calls for reduced petrol and diesel consumption in India amid rising global fuel crisis from Middle East conflict
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has appealed to the citizens of India to reduce their consumption of petrol and diesel because of supply disruptions caused by the ongoing war in the Middle East. F...
